title: Gladiolus thunbergii
intro: Gladiolus thunbergii, also known as the Naked Lady or Autumn Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the iris family, characterized by its striking white blooms with a yellow center and sword-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gladiolus thunbergii include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Gladiolus thunbergii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Gladiolus thunbergii is classified within the family Iridaceae and the subfamily Ixioideae. The genus Gladiolus is further divided into several subgenera. It belongs to the tribe Ixieae within the Ixioideae subfamily. The species Gladiolus thunbergii is native to South Africa. This plant is also known as Gladiolus thunbergii var. thunbergii.
